introSection: ArieForce One is a steel roller coaster at Fun Spot America Atlanta in Fayetteville, Georgia, USA. It was manufactured by Rocky Mountain Construction, designed by Joe Draves, and opened in 2023.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Fun Spot announced that it was partnering with Rocky Mountain Construction on a new roller coaster on May 25, 2021. A photograph of track fabrication shared by Fun Spot on June 17 revealed that the ride would be an I-Box installation.

The name ArieForce One, a tribute to the founder of Fun Spot John Arie, was revealed in a press release on November 15, 2021, and announced the following day at the 2021 IAAPA Expo in Orlando, Florida, USA.

A groundbreaking ceremony was held on January 6, 2022. Construction then began the following month. In March, the first track pieces had already arrived.

Testing began in December 2022. ArieForce One opened on March 31, 2023.

sections.title: Etymology
sections.content: The ride is named after Fun Spot CEO John Arie, Jr.'s father, John Arie Sr.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: The park had originally hoped to have a theme based on one of Atlanta's professional sports teams, however, this did not work out. Instead, the roller coaster would have an American theme.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: One rail is painted blue, the other red, and the supports are painted white.

fullText:
ArieForce One is a steel roller coaster at Fun Spot America Atlanta in Fayetteville, Georgia, USA. It was manufactured by Rocky Mountain Construction, designed by Joe Draves, and opened in 2023.

Fun Spot announced that it was partnering with Rocky Mountain Construction on a new roller coaster on May 25, 2021. A photograph of track fabrication shared by Fun Spot on June 17 revealed that the ride would be an I-Box installation.

The name ArieForce One, a tribute to the founder of Fun Spot John Arie, was revealed in a press release on November 15, 2021, and announced the following day at the 2021 IAAPA Expo in Orlando, Florida, USA.

A groundbreaking ceremony was held on January 6, 2022. Construction then began the following month. In March, the first track pieces had already arrived.

Testing began in December 2022. ArieForce One opened on March 31, 2023.

The ride is named after Fun Spot CEO John Arie, Jr.'s father, John Arie Sr.

The park had originally hoped to have a theme based on one of Atlanta's professional sports teams, however, this did not work out. Instead, the roller coaster would have an American theme.

One rail is painted blue, the other red, and the supports are painted white.
